summary: Iteration 4 (4 new features on top of iteration 3): AI Chat live streaming, Module Transition overlay, Waitlist Admin dashboard, OS Memory (localStorage). Backend: 12/12 pytest pass including new admin login (wrong pw 401 / correct pw 200 with token+expires_at), admin leads (401 without/with-bad token, 200 with valid bearer), and /api/chat/stream SSE with real Claude Sonnet 4.6 tokens plus 422 on empty text. Frontend: (a) AI Chat streams real Claude content ('Today's Pipeline — Quick Brief …'); Send button switches to 'Streaming' with spinning BlackHoleMark; second turn also streamed. Suggestion chip 'chat-suggest-give' visible on empty chat and sends. (b) ModuleTransition: switching nav-dashboard → nav-tasks flips overlay to class 'os-transition os-transition-on' briefly, then fades within 520ms — verified programmatically. (c) OS Memory: navigating to nav-analytics writes {activeModule:'analytics'} to localStorage 'xenia.os.state'; after reload Analytics module is auto-active. Selecting 'Companies' in the palette adds it to paletteRecents; after reload the palette opens with 'RECENTS' section and Companies at the top. (d) Admin: /admin loads admin-root + admin-login-form; wrong pw shows 'Incorrect password' toast (401); correct pw ('xenia-admin-2026') shows admin-dashboard + admin-leads-table with 17 admin-lead-<id> rows; admin-refresh works; token stored in xenia.admin.token persists across reload (stays logged in); admin-logout returns to login form. Regression: POST /api/access still returns 200 on new email; homepage hero + XeniaOS render as before; only expected console error is the intentional 401 from the wrong-password login attempt.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py — /api/admin/login uses hmac.compare_digest for constant-time comparison and PyJWT HS256 with 24h TTL; require_admin dependency cleanly distinguishes ExpiredSignatureError vs InvalidTokenError. Good.

server.py — /api/chat/stream uses emergentintegrations LlmChat with a stable session_id from the client, so multi-turn context accumulates server-side without us re-sending history. Verified end-to-end.
OSContext.jsx — persists {activeModule, paletteRecents} to localStorage key 'xenia.os.state'; loadPersisted is guarded and swallows JSON parse errors. Reload restores activeModule correctly.

CommandPalette.jsx — recents pre-pended when query is empty; rememberPaletteHit called before the item's action fires, so even navigation to modules that unmount the palette still persists. Nicely done.
ModuleTransition.jsx — firstRun ref prevents the overlay from flashing on initial mount; cleanup clears the hide timer on unmount. Solid.
Admin.jsx — expires_at converted to epoch seconds and re-checked on mount via readToken(); a 401 on /api/admin/leads triggers logout automatically. Good defensive UX.

Non-blocking: server.py — /api/chat/stream errors are swallowed into an SSE 'event: error' line but the HTTP status is already 200 (streaming has started). Consider surfacing model-key/permission failures via a pre-stream 500 when possible.
Non-blocking: OSContext.jsx — savePersisted runs on every activeModule change including the very first setActiveModule from the persisted default, effectively a no-op but a minor extra write. Fine.
Non-blocking: Admin.jsx — no lockout / rate-limit on failed logins yet; a brute-force wrapper (or captcha) would be prudent before public exposure.
seed_data_creation: A handful of test_<uuid>@example.com AccessRequest rows per test run (harmless waitlist entries; safe to ignore or purge).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 4 new features are live and pass. Admin route: /admin (JWT in localStorage keys xenia.admin.token / xenia.admin.exp). OS state: xenia.os.state ({activeModule, paletteRecents<=8}). Chat session: xenia.chat.session. Always clear localStorage before persistence tests. When measuring AI-chat streaming in Playwright, do NOT snapshot text immediately after Enter — wait for the send button to lose the 'Streaming' label AND for the last assistant bubble to be non-empty; my first test grabbed the bubble too early and saw only '**'. The screenshot confirmed the full Claude paragraph arrived correctly.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional issues. Test-side note: initial AI Chat text-length assertion misfired because the assistant bubble was sampled at t≈0ms into the stream (only the leading '**' bold marker was present); a screenshot immediately after showed the full 'Today's Pipeline — Quick Brief …' paragraph rendered. Backend SSE test (test_chat_stream_returns_sse_tokens) confirms real tokens flow, so this was a test-timing artefact not a product bug.
